summary: The bridge is published in standalone and offscreen bootstrap, but the extension Terminal tab still cannot access it because the side-panel shell runs in a different window context with no `__slicc_agent` proxy.

issues.description: Extension mode still leaves the user-facing Terminal tab without an AgentBridge. `publishAgentBridge()` only writes `globalThis.__slicc_agent` in the offscreen document, but the extension Terminal tab runs its own WasmShell in the side-panel window, where `agent-command` still falls back to `agent: orchestrator bridge not available`.
issues.evidence: `packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:278-339` creates the extension side-panel WasmShell for the Terminal tab; `packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:1158-1163` publishes `__slicc_agent` only in the standalone branch, not `mainExtension()`. `packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen.ts:71-80` publishes the hook only in the offscreen document. `packages/chrome-extension/CLAUDE.md:50-55` documents that the side panel shell and offscreen shell do not share window globals, and `packages/webapp/src/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.ts:215-218` errors when the hook is missing.
issues.suggestedFix: Provide the bridge to the extension panel shell as well (for example by proxying spawn requests through `OffscreenClient`/`chrome.runtime` or by publishing a panel-local bridge hook), and add an integration test that exercises the extension side-panel Terminal tab path rather than only the single-context helper harness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/architecture.md` describes `globalThis.__slicc_agent` as a generic extension hook published from `offscreen.ts`, but it does not mention that the extension has separate side-panel and offscreen shells with isolated window globals.
sharedStateObservations.why: That omission makes the current plan look sufficient for extension support even though the panel Terminal tab cannot see the offscreen global. Capturing the dual-context constraint in the mission architecture would help future workers avoid the same mistake.
